rationale: Review 1 catches a confirmed additional scope/design flaw — AlgPoints being k-points-only (line 45) rather than the functor-of-points the roadmap prescribes — that Review 2 actively misidentifies as 'plausibly-legitimate'; acting on Review 1 fixes this while acting on Review 2 leaves it broken. Both reviews correctly identify the True-field stubs and naïve IsUnipotent as out-of-scope, so that ground is a tie.
